my name is
amy lennon and i am
from long island new york i'm 34 years
old
i am currently single i live
near the east end of long island near
southampton where i
am a physical education teacher i coach
the
cross country and track mail team
as well as train and
at crossfit 11968 so that's my little
bio and i'll tell you a little bit more
about myself and
a little bit growing up
i was upside down more than i was right
side up i was a gymnast
who eventually grew to be five foot ten
with a six foot
four wingspan um with that body it's a
little bit more difficult and
challenging
however it never stopped me and found a
sport that was more suited for me
pole vaulting i graduated high school
winning
nationals and going to the first ever
olympic trials for the women's pole
vault
i then later went to the university of
arizona on a full scholarship
my sophomore year i won nationals
setting the collegiate record
and ranking me fourth in the world that
collegiate record held for 12 years
later on my junior year i transferred to
the university of kansas
where i won the ncaa my senior year
and now in both
university of kansas and university of
arizona hall sports hall of fame
life after pole vaulting is
quite adventurous i travel i do many
different sports to keep me active
and in shape i get to be back on the
mountain skiing and get in the water
surfing
and do all the activities that i loved
doing when i grew up i'm currently
an ambassador for surf tech and bark
surf
paddle boards i love to paddle and race
paddle boards i get to travel a bunch
and
be out in the open ocean and go you know
surf um surfing is my passion it's what
i love to do
if i could trade in all my athletic
ability to just be a surfer i would
um the ocean is my happy place and
it's where i feel at home
i had no choice but to love the ocean
but i also had to have a love for the
mountains my mom and dad would take me
up
skiing and snowboarding and we travel
out to colorado and utah and
um i have now uh
the same love that they did when they
were teaching me
how to ski training as an elite pole
vaulter was definitely challenging
and very demanding but life after pole
vaulting has not stopped me if anything
i am more prepared now
since i'm doing a variety of things
including teaching my little phys ed
students
all different sports crossfit surfing
skiing
anything that gets me upside down or
challenges me is
always exciting i'm excited for our new
challenge
with april becky and arielle we all were
individual athletes and now together our
forces unite

videos.title: World's best cross-country skiers gather at Stifel Loppet Cup
videos.description:
The Stifel Loppet Cup is the first World Cup cross-country race to be held on U.S. soil in more than 20 years.

videos.transcript:
but the weather did make for a perfect
backdrop for a cross-country skiing race
today at Lapham park in Delafield it's
so fun to be on the snow the 2019 Lapham
Lobeck got a little help from Mother
Nature soft mushy bass have real snow
the Lapham peak loop is just more than a
mile long and usually relies on man-made
snow to keep skiers going but the fresh
powder can also have a downside but the
fresh snow is pretty nice but I was kind
of struggling on some of the hills there
the skiers weren't just gliding along
today they were racing for some it's a
test of endurance I'm usually really
excited for a race I enjoy racing to see
how far I can push myself for others
it's a case of sibling rivalry just got
to beat my sister
make sure the race also serves as a
fundraiser for the park hoping to add
another half mile of trail in the woods
it's been a lot of years of working on
it but it's a wonderful thing now that
we've got it all I hope to get started
on the edition of that in the fall if
they raise 160 thousand dollars so some
perfect weather for the skiers today
